title: Measurements of neutron capture cross-sections for nuclides of interest in decommissioning: 45Sc, 63Cu, 64Zn, 109Ag, and 113In

x4freetext: The graphite thermal column (TC-Pn) in KUR.
The nominal neutron flux intensity of TC-Pn is
The AUTHORs confirmed that the higher part of the TC-Pn
was well thermalized on the basis of Westcott
convention using neutron flux monitors: Au-197, Co-59,
and Mo-98. (See Fig. 5 in the REFERENCE.)
Because the neutron flux in the TC-Pn is well
The gamma-ray peak efficiencies of the Ge detector
were measured using a Eu-152 source and a mixed source
The uncertainty in the source radioactivity was 1.5%
for both of the standard sources.
The reaction rates of the target reactions were
obtained from the yields of the gamma rays emitted
The thermal-neutron fluxes were derived using the
reaction rates and the thermal cross section of the
- For Sc-45, Cu-63, Ag-109 and In-113:
The capture cross sections were derived using the
reaction rates and the thermal-neutron fluxes.
The thermal capture cross sections for monitors and
the g-factors were taken from the REL-REF.
For Mo-98, the thermal capture cross section was taken
from an AUTHOR`s unpublished previous measurement.
Self-shielding coefficients for thermal neutrons were
calculated from neutron capture cross-sections and
area densities of the sample and the monitors.
The coincidence summing effect was estimated from the
peak-to-total ratios and the peak efficiencies.
Thermal capture cross sections of the monitors were
assumed to be well known to determine the thermal
